Paracetamol 500mg.overview: Paracetamol 500mg is one of the most widely used over-the-counter analgesic and antipyretic medicines in India.
Paracetamol 500mg.dosage: Adults: 1-2 tablets every 4-6 hours. Max 8 tablets in 24 hours.

history.intro: Paracetamol has been a cornerstone of pain management for over a century.

timeline.event: First synthesized by Harmon Northrop Morse.
Marketed as Tylenol in the US.

Omron BP Monitor HEM-7120.overview: Fully automatic upper arm blood pressure monitor for home use.
Omron BP Monitor HEM-7120.instructions: Wrap cuff around upper arm. Press START. Remain still during measurement.

Dabur Chyawanprash 500g.overview: Traditional Ayurvedic immunity booster with 40+ herbs.

Betadine Solution 100ml.overview: Broad-spectrum antiseptic for minor cuts, wounds, and burns.
Betadine Solution 100ml.howToUse: Clean area. Apply directly with cotton. Allow to dry. Repeat 1-3 times daily.

articles.title: Advances in mRNA Vaccine Technology: From COVID-19 to Cancer Therapeutics
articles.body: The rapid development of mRNA vaccines during COVID-19 has opened new frontiers in vaccine technology. Researchers are now exploring mRNA platforms for cancer immunotherapy, with early-phase trials showing promising results in melanoma and pancreatic cancer.

articles.title: Pharmacokinetics of Paracetamol in Pediatric Populations
articles.body: This systematic review analyzes pharmacokinetic parameters of paracetamol in children aged 0-17 years. Key findings indicate hepatic metabolism matures significantly between ages 2-6, affecting optimal dosing strategies.

articles.title: Phase III Trial: Novel GLP-1 Receptor Agonist for Type 2 Diabetes
articles.body: Multi-center Phase III trial of a novel GLP-1 receptor agonist demonstrates significant HbA1c improvements and weight reduction compared to existing treatments. 4,200 participants across 180 sites.

articles.title: Antibiotic Resistance in India: Current Trends and Strategies
articles.body: Antimicrobial resistance poses a critical public health threat in India. This article reviews ICMR surveillance data documenting rising resistance in E. coli and Klebsiella pneumoniae.

articles.title: FDA Safety Alert: Updated Black Box Warning for Fluoroquinolones
articles.body: The FDA has issued updated safety labeling for fluoroquinolone antibiotics highlighting increased risks of tendon rupture, peripheral neuropathy, and CNS effects.

articles.title: AI-Powered Drug Discovery: Machine Learning for Target Identification
articles.body: Deep learning models trained on protein structure databases are accelerating target identification and lead optimization in pharmaceutical research.

articles.title: Comprehensive Guide to Managing Hypertension
articles.body: Hypertension affects over 200 million adults in India. This guide covers ACE inhibitors, ARBs, calcium channel blockers, and evidence-based lifestyle modifications.

articles.title: CDSCO Guidelines for Biosimilar Approval in India
articles.body: New CDSCO framework streamlines biosimilar drug approval while maintaining safety standards through comparative analytical studies and abbreviated clinical trials.

articles.title: CRISPR-Cas9 Gene Therapy: First Approved Treatment for Sickle Cell Disease
articles.body: The first CRISPR-based gene therapy has received regulatory approval for sickle cell disease. This landmark treatment modifies patients' own hematopoietic stem cells to produce functional hemoglobin.

articles.title: Metformin Beyond Diabetes: Anti-Aging and Anti-Cancer Properties
articles.body: Emerging evidence suggests metformin activates AMPK pathways associated with cellular longevity and may have anti-cancer properties through inhibition of mTOR signaling.

articles.title: Phase II Results: Oral Insulin Capsule Shows Promise
articles.body: An oral insulin capsule with enteric coating has shown 40% bioavailability in Phase II trials, potentially replacing injectable insulin for Type 2 diabetes patients.

articles.title: Global Tuberculosis Report 2025: India's Progress and Challenges
articles.body: India accounts for 26% of global TB cases. The WHO 2025 report notes a 12% reduction in incidence since 2020 but highlights drug-resistant TB as a growing concern.

articles.title: Adverse Events with Proton Pump Inhibitors: Long-Term Safety Review
articles.body: Long-term PPI use is associated with increased risks of kidney disease, bone fractures, C. difficile infection, and vitamin B12 deficiency. Deprescribing strategies are recommended.

articles.title: Wearable Biosensors for Real-Time Drug Monitoring
articles.body: Sweat-based biosensors can now monitor therapeutic drug levels in real-time, enabling personalized dosing for drugs with narrow therapeutic windows like lithium and vancomycin.

articles.title: Treatment Guidelines for Dengue Fever: 2025 Update
articles.body: Updated WHO guidelines for dengue management emphasize fluid replacement therapy, platelet monitoring, and the contraindication of NSAIDs. A new classification system stratifies patients by severity.

articles.title: India's PLI Scheme Impact on API Manufacturing
articles.body: The Production Linked Incentive scheme has attracted ₹15,000 crore in investment for domestic API manufacturing, reducing India's dependence on Chinese imports for 53 critical drug intermediates.

articles.title: Psychedelic-Assisted Therapy: Psilocybin for Treatment-Resistant Depression
articles.body: A Phase III trial of psilocybin-assisted therapy for treatment-resistant depression has enrolled 800 participants across 50 centers. Preliminary data shows 60% remission rates after two sessions.

articles.title: Pharmacogenomics in Indian Populations: CYP2D6 Variants
articles.body: Genetic variations in CYP2D6 enzyme among Indian ethnic groups significantly affect metabolism of codeine, tamoxifen, and several antidepressants. Population-specific dosing guidelines are needed.

articles.title: COVID-19 Long Haulers: Neurological Complications and Treatment
articles.body: Post-COVID neurological complications including brain fog, chronic fatigue, and dysautonomia affect 10-30% of COVID survivors. Multidisciplinary rehabilitation approaches show the most promise.

articles.title: Digital Therapeutics: FDA-Approved Apps for Substance Use Disorders
articles.body: The FDA has approved three digital therapeutic applications for substance use disorders that deliver cognitive behavioral therapy through smartphone apps, representing a new category of prescription digital medicines.

news.title: India's Pharmaceutical Exports Cross $28 Billion in FY2025-26
news.body: India's pharmaceutical exports have surpassed $28 billion for the first time, driven by generic medicines demand in Africa and Southeast Asia. The PLI scheme contributed significantly.

news.title: WHO Prequalifies New Malaria Vaccine R21 for Children
news.body: WHO has prequalified R21/Matrix-M malaria vaccine for children aged 5-36 months. 75% efficacy demonstrated at $2-4 per dose cost.

news.title: Cipla Launches AI Drug Interaction Checker for Pharmacists
news.body: Cipla has launched an AI tool covering 15,000+ drug combinations for real-time interaction alerts and dosage conflict detection.

news.title: DCGI Approves First Indigenous mRNA Vaccine Platform
news.body: India's first domestically developed mRNA platform approved for Phase I/II trials, targeting seasonal influenza with pandemic adaptability.

news.title: Amazon Pharmacy Expands Same-Day Delivery to 15 New Cities
news.body: Amazon Pharmacy now covers 40 Indian cities with 4-hour delivery for essential medicines and healthcare products.

news.title: Sun Pharma Reports Record ₹52,000 Crore Q3 Revenue
news.body: Sun Pharmaceutical Industries reports record quarterly revenue driven by US specialty products and India branded formulations.

news.title: Dr. Reddy's Acquires European Biosimilar Portfolio for $600M
news.body: Dr. Reddy's Laboratories has acquired a portfolio of 8 biosimilar products from a European pharmaceutical company for $600 million, strengthening its presence in the EU biologics market.

news.title: NPPA Caps Prices of 128 Essential Medicines for 2026
news.body: The National Pharmaceutical Pricing Authority has announced ceiling prices for 128 essential medicines including antibiotics, cardiac drugs, and anti-diabetics effective January 2026.

news.title: Zydus Lifesciences Gets FDA Approval for Generic Ozempic
news.body: Zydus Lifesciences has received US FDA approval for a generic version of semaglutide injection (Ozempic), making the diabetes and weight-loss drug more accessible.

news.title: India Mandates QR Codes on All Pharmaceutical Packaging
news.body: Starting April 2026, all pharmaceutical products sold in India must carry QR codes linking to authentication databases to combat counterfeit medicines.

news.title: Biocon Biologics Launches India's Most Affordable Insulin
news.body: Biocon Biologics has launched a biosimilar insulin glargine at ₹325 per pen, making it the most affordable long-acting insulin available in India's retail pharmacy market.

news.title: PharmEasy IPO Faces Regulatory Scrutiny Over Data Practices
news.body: PharmEasy's planned IPO has been delayed after SEBI raised concerns about the company's health data collection practices and prescription handling procedures.

news.title: WHO Declares End of Mpox Global Health Emergency
news.body: The World Health Organization has officially declared the end of the mpox public health emergency of international concern, citing sustained decline in global case counts.

news.title: Tata 1mg Integrates AI Prescription Verification System
news.body: Tata 1mg has deployed an AI-powered prescription verification system that automatically checks for drug interactions, contraindications, and dosage errors before dispensing.

news.title: India's First Cell and Gene Therapy Hub Opens in Hyderabad
news.body: India's first dedicated cell and gene therapy manufacturing hub has been inaugurated in Hyderabad's Genome Valley, with capacity to produce CAR-T cell therapies for blood cancers.
